message:
feat: tiered pane update rates for adaptive polling (bd-9dp)

Adds pane_tiers.rs with:
- PaneTier enum (Active/Thinking/Idle/Background/Dormant) with poll intervals
- TierConfig for customizable intervals and thresholds
- PaneTierClassifier with time-based classification and instant promotion
- Backpressure multiplier integration (Green/Yellow/Red/Black)
- Per-pane state tracking with transition metrics
- 200-pane scale test, thread safety test
- 29 tests passing

Expected impact: 80-90% reduction in mux protocol requests for typical
agent swarm workloads (250/s → ~30/s).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-8q4e","title":"FTUI-02.3 Implement temporary compatibility adapter (legacy TUI facade)","description":"## Background\nMigration will be incremental; a thin compatibility layer prevents half-migrated callsites from breaking.\n\n## Deliverables\n- adapter boundary isolating legacy ratatui-facing code\n- migration shims with clear deletion criteria\n- compile-time warnings/notes for remaining legacy callsites\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- adapter compiles and allows partial migration without behavior loss\n- deletion criteria are explicitly documented.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:44.952934319Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T01:18:02.947472549Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T01:18:02.947403902Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-8q4e","depends_on_id":"bd-1utb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-985.3","title":"Analytics CLI: wa analytics summary/breakdown/export commands","description":"# Analytics CLI commands

## Purpose
Provide user-friendly CLI commands for viewing and exporting usage analytics.

## Commands

### wa analytics
Default summary view:
```bash
wa analytics

# Output
╭────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ Usage Analytics (Last 7 Days)                          │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Total Tokens: 1,234,567                                │
│ Estimated Cost: $12.34                                 │
│ Rate Limits Hit: 3                                     │
│ Workflows Run: 45                                      │
│                                                        │
│ Trend: ▁▂▃▅▇▆▄ (tokens/day)                           │
│        ↑15% vs previous week                           │
╰────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

### wa analytics breakdown
```bash
wa analytics breakdown [--by agent|account|workflow] [--period 7d|30d|90d]

# By agent
Agent         Tokens      Cost     % of Total
Codex         856,000     $8.56    69%
Claude Code   378,567     $3.78    31%

# By workflow
Workflow              Runs  Avg Tokens  Total Cost
handle_usage_limits   32    5,000       $1.60
handle_compaction     13    2,500       $0.33
```

### wa analytics trend
```bash
wa analytics trend [--metric tokens|cost|rate_limits] [--period 7d|30d]

# Output (sparkline chart)
Tokens per Day (Last 30 Days)
▁▂▂▃▃▄▅▅▆▆▇▇█▇▇▆▆▅▅▄▄▃▃▂▂▁▁▁▁▁
Jan 1                            Jan 30
Min: 10,000  Max: 100,000  Avg: 45,000
```

### wa analytics export
```bash
wa analytics export [--format csv|json] [--period 7d|30d|90d] [--output file]

# Examples
wa analytics export --format csv --period 30d --output usage.csv
wa analytics export --format json --period 7d
```

### wa analytics alerts
```bash
wa analytics alerts [--list|--add|--remove]

# List alerts
ID    Metric     Threshold   Status
1     cost       $50/day     OK
2     tokens     1M/day      TRIGGERED (1.2M)

# Add alert
wa analytics alerts --add --metric cost --threshold 100 --period day
```

## Robot Mode
```bash
wa robot analytics summary
wa robot analytics breakdown --by agent
wa robot analytics export --format json
```

## Testing
- CLI argument parsing tests
- Output formatting tests
- Export format validation tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] wa analytics shows summary
- [ ] wa analytics breakdown works for all dimensions
- [ ] wa analytics trend shows sparkline charts
- [ ] wa analytics export produces valid CSV/JSON
- [ ] wa analytics alerts configurable
- [ ] Robot mode produces valid JSON
- [ ] Tests cover all commands","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:51:26.822292305Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-06T05:01:38.158484824Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T05:01:38.158335617Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-985.3","depends_on_id":"bd-nu4.3.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-985.4","title":"Proactive alerts: threshold monitoring and notification triggers","description":"# Proactive alerts

## Purpose
Alert users before they hit limits or exceed budgets, preventing surprises and enabling proactive optimization.

## Alert Types

### 1. Cost Threshold Alerts
```rust
pub struct CostAlert {
    id: AlertId,
    threshold: Decimal,      // e.g., $50.00
    period: AlertPeriod,     // Day, Week, Month
    current_value: Decimal,
    percent_of_threshold: f32,
}

pub enum AlertLevel {
    Info,      // 50% of threshold
    Warning,   // 75% of threshold
    Critical,  // 90% of threshold
    Exceeded,  // 100%+ of threshold
}
```

### 2. Token Usage Alerts
```rust
pub struct TokenAlert {
    threshold: u64,
    period: AlertPeriod,
    per_agent: Option<AgentType>,
}
```

### 3. Rate Limit Frequency Alerts
```rust
pub struct RateLimitAlert {
    max_hits: u32,     // e.g., 5 per hour
    period: Duration,
}
```

### 4. Account Balance Alerts
```rust
pub struct AccountBalanceAlert {
    account_id: String,
    min_percent_remaining: f32,  // e.g., 15%
}
```

## Alert Configuration
```toml
# wa.toml
[analytics.alerts]
enabled = true

[[analytics.alerts.rules]]
metric = "cost"
threshold = 50.0
period = "day"
notify = ["terminal"]  # or "webhook", "email"

[[analytics.alerts.rules]]
metric = "account_balance"
threshold = 15  # percent
account = "*"   # all accounts
notify = ["terminal", "webhook"]
```

## Alert Monitoring Service
```rust
pub struct AlertMonitor {
    rules: Vec<AlertRule>,
    storage: StorageHandle,
    notifiers: Vec<Box<dyn Notifier>>,
}

impl AlertMonitor {
    pub async fn check_alerts(&self) -> Vec<TriggeredAlert> {
        let mut triggered = vec![];
        
        for rule in &self.rules {
            let current = self.get_current_value(&rule).await?;
            if let Some(alert) = rule.check(current) {
                triggered.push(alert);
            }
        }
        
        triggered
    }
    
    pub async fn notify_triggered(&self, alerts: &[TriggeredAlert]) -> Result<()> {
        for alert in alerts {
            for notifier in &self.notifiers {
                notifier.notify(alert).await?;
            }
        }
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

## Notification Channels

### Terminal (CLI output)
```
⚠️  ALERT: Daily cost threshold approaching
    Current: $42.50 / $50.00 (85%)
    Recommendation: Consider pausing non-critical workflows
```

### Status Command Integration
```bash
wa status

# Shows alerts at top
⚠️  1 active alert: Daily cost at 85% of $50 limit

Panes: 3 active...
```

## Testing
- Unit tests for threshold calculations
- Integration tests with mock metrics
- Tests for each notification channel

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cost alerts trigger at configured thresholds
- [ ] Token alerts work per-agent and global
- [ ] Rate limit frequency tracked and alerted
- [ ] Account balance alerts work
- [ ] Terminal notifications clear and visible
- [ ] wa status shows active alerts
- [ ] Tests cover all alert types","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:51:45.285325644Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-06T04:37:30.392259592Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T04:37:30.392123379Z"}
-{"id":"bd-9dp","title":"Tiered update rates — aggressive backoff for idle and background panes","description":"## Goal
Implement intelligent tiered polling rates for pane output capture, aggressively reducing polling frequency for idle and background panes while maintaining responsive capture for active panes.

## Background & Motivation
wa's CaptureScheduler (tailer.rs) currently polls all panes at a uniform rate. With 50+ panes, this means 250+ mux protocol requests per second, even when most panes are idle (waiting for rate limits, thinking, etc.). This wastes CPU, network, and mux server capacity.

AI agent workloads have a distinctive pattern: at any given time, maybe 5-10 panes are actively producing output while the other 40+ are idle. wa already has pattern detection that identifies pane states (idle, rate-limited, thinking, active). We should use this to dramatically reduce polling for non-active panes.

## Relationship to Other Optimizations
This bead works in concert with two complementary beads:
- **wa-x4rq (Notification Coalescing)**: bd-9dp sets WHEN to poll each pane. wa-x4rq batches the NOTIFICATIONS that trigger those polls. Together: bd-9dp reduces baseline poll rate by 80-90%, and wa-x4rq further reduces redundant captures within each tier's interval.
- **wa-3bin (Smart Priority)**: bd-9dp implements the mechanical tier system. wa-3bin provides the CLASSIFICATION that maps panes to tiers (using pattern detection + output rates). bd-9dp can work standalone with simple time-based classification, but wa-3bin makes it much smarter.
- **backpressure.rs (existing)**: Backpressure's tiers (Green/Yellow/Red/Black) are about SYSTEM LOAD. bd-9dp's tiers are about PANE ACTIVITY. They're orthogonal: under backpressure (Red/Black), bd-9dp's intervals can be further multiplied to reduce load.

## Technical Design

### Tier Definitions
```rust
// Location: crates/wa-core/src/tailer.rs (modify existing CaptureScheduler)

pub enum PaneTier {
    Active,      // Producing output -- poll every 200ms (current default)
    Thinking,    // Agent is processing -- poll every 2s
    Idle,        // No output for >30s -- poll every 5s
    Background,  // Minimized/hidden tab -- poll every 10s
    Dormant,     // Rate-limited or paused >5min -- poll every 30s
}

impl PaneTier {
    pub fn poll_interval(&self) -> Duration {
        match self {
            Self::Active => Duration::from_millis(200),
            Self::Thinking => Duration::from_secs(2),
            Self::Idle => Duration::from_secs(5),
            Self::Background => Duration::from_secs(10),
            Self::Dormant => Duration::from_secs(30),
        }
    }

    /// Under backpressure, multiply intervals by this factor
    pub fn backpressure_multiplier(&self, tier: BackpressureTier) -> f64 {
        match tier {
            BackpressureTier::Green => 1.0,
            BackpressureTier::Yellow => 1.5,
            BackpressureTier::Red => 3.0,
            BackpressureTier::Black => 10.0,
        }
    }
}
```

### Tier Classification Logic
Simple version (standalone, without wa-3bin):
```rust
fn classify_pane(&self, pane_id: PaneId) -> PaneTier {
    let last_output = self.last_output_time(pane_id);
    let elapsed = Instant::now() - last_output;
    let state = self.pattern_engine.current_state(pane_id);

    match state {
        AgentState::RateLimited => PaneTier::Dormant,
        AgentState::WaitingForInput if elapsed > Duration::from_secs(300) => PaneTier::Dormant,
        AgentState::WaitingForInput => PaneTier::Idle,
        AgentState::Thinking => PaneTier::Thinking,
        _ if elapsed > Duration::from_secs(30) => PaneTier::Idle,
        _ => PaneTier::Active,
    }
}
```

With wa-3bin (enhanced classification):
```rust
fn classify_pane_enhanced(&self, pane_id: PaneId, classifier: &PriorityClassifier) -> PaneTier {
    match classifier.classify(pane_id) {
        PanePriority::Critical | PanePriority::High => PaneTier::Active,
        PanePriority::Medium => PaneTier::Thinking,
        PanePriority::Low => PaneTier::Idle,
        PanePriority::Background => PaneTier::Dormant,
    }
}
```

### Instant Promotion
When a pane produces new output, it's immediately promoted to Active tier:
```rust
fn on_pane_output(&mut self, pane_id: PaneId) {
    self.tier_map.insert(pane_id, PaneTier::Active);
    self.last_output_time.insert(pane_id, Instant::now());
    self.wake_scheduler.notify_one();
}
```

### Integration with CaptureScheduler
```rust
async fn capture_loop(&mut self) {
    loop {
        let next = self.pane_timers.iter()
            .min_by_key(|(_, timer)| timer.deadline())
            .map(|(id, _)| *id);

        if let Some(pane_id) = next {
            let timer = &self.pane_timers[&pane_id];
            tokio::select! {
                _ = timer.tick() => {
                    self.capture_pane(pane_id).await;
                    let tier = self.classify_pane(pane_id);
                    // Apply backpressure multiplier
                    let bp_mult = tier.backpressure_multiplier(
                        self.backpressure.current_tier()
                    );
                    let interval = tier.poll_interval().mul_f64(bp_mult);
                    self.pane_timers.get_mut(&pane_id)
                        .unwrap()
                        .set_interval(interval);
                }
                _ = self.wake_scheduler.notified() => {
                    continue;
                }
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### Configuration
```toml
[capture.tiers]
active_ms = 200
thinking_ms = 2000
idle_ms = 5000
background_ms = 10000
dormant_ms = 30000
idle_threshold_secs = 30
dormant_threshold_secs = 300
```

## Existing Code References
- CaptureScheduler: crates/wa-core/src/tailer.rs (adaptive polling, token bucket)
- TailerConfig: poll intervals, backoff settings
- BackpressureMonitor: crates/wa-core/src/backpressure.rs (system load tiers -- orthogonal to activity tiers)

## Expected Impact
- 80-90% reduction in mux protocol requests (from ~250/s to ~30/s for typical workload)
- Proportional CPU reduction on both wa and mux server
- Mux server lock contention dramatically reduced
- Active panes remain just as responsive

## Dependencies
None -- standalone improvement. Enhanced by wa-3bin (smart classification) and complemented by wa-x4rq (notification coalescing).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Panes classified into correct tiers based on output activity
- Active panes polled at 200ms (no regression)
- Idle panes polled at 5s (25x reduction)
- Dormant panes polled at 30s (150x reduction)
- Instant promotion to Active when output detected
- Backpressure multiplier applied on top of tier intervals
- Configuration via wa.toml
- Metrics: per-tier pane counts, total requests/s, backpressure multiplier

## Estimated Effort
3-4 hours implementation, 1 hour testing

## Benchmark & Property Testing Requirements

### Criterion Benchmarks
Add `benches/tiered_update.rs` using criterion:
- **tier_assignment_latency**: Benchmark classify_pane() for 50, 100, 200 panes. Target: <1us per pane classification (must be fast since it runs on every poll cycle).
- **tier_assignment_with_pattern_engine**: Benchmark classify_pane() when it queries the pattern engine for AgentState. Measure the overhead of pattern engine lookups.
- **scheduler_tick_overhead**: Benchmark a full capture_loop tick (select next pane, classify, update timer) without actual mux I/O. Target: <10us per tick for 100 panes.
- **instant_promotion_latency**: Benchmark the time from on_pane_output() call to the pane being reclassified as Active. Target: <5us.

### Proptest
Add `tests/proptest_tiers.rs`:
- **tier_transition_invariants**: For any arbitrary sequence of (pane_id, elapsed_time, agent_state) tuples (proptest generates), assert that tier transitions are monotonically correct: Active -> Thinking -> Idle -> Dormant as elapsed time increases (never skipping tiers or going backwards without new output).
- **backpressure_multiplier_monotonicity**: For any PaneTier and any BackpressureTier, the effective interval (tier.poll_interval * backpressure_multiplier) must be monotonically non-decreasing as backpressure increases (Green <= Yellow <= Red <= Black).
- **instant_promotion_always_works**: For any pane in any tier, calling on_pane_output() must result in PaneTier::Active on the next classify_pane() call.

## Cross-References
- **wa-3bin** (Smart pane priority classification): wa-3bin provides the intelligent PanePriority classifier that feeds into bd-9dp's tier system via classify_pane_enhanced(). When wa-3bin is implemented, bd-9dp should switch from time-based classification to priority-based classification for more accurate tier assignment.
- **wa-283h4.8** (Entropy-aware scheduling): The entropy-aware scheduler uses information-theoretic measures to decide which panes have the most "surprising" (high-entropy) output. This complements bd-9dp's tier system: entropy-aware scheduling can promote panes with high-entropy output to Active tier even if they haven't produced output recently (e.g., a pane that just received a critical error message after being idle).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:20:32.187945Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:49:00.397756Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9dp","depends_on_id":"wa-3cyp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:37:32.740689Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}

When wa-3bin is implemented, bd-9dp should switch from time-based classification to priority-based classification for more accurate tier assignment.
- **wa-283h4.8** (Entropy-aware scheduling): The entropy-aware scheduler uses information-theoretic measures to decide which panes have the most "surprising" (high-entropy) output. This complements bd-9dp's tier system: entropy-aware scheduling can promote panes with high-entropy output to Active tier even if they haven't produced output recently (e.g., a pane that just received a critical error message after being idle).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:20:32.187945Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T23:59:04.46484-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9dp","depends_on_id":"wa-3cyp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:37:32.740689Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}
 {"id":"bd-9l5","title":"Scheduled backup system: cron-style automatic backups with retention","description":"# Task: Scheduled Backup System

## Goal
Provide peace of mind through automatic, hands-off backups.

## Configuration
```toml
[backup.scheduled]
enabled = true

# Schedule (cron-like or simple keywords)
schedule = "daily"  # or "0 3 * * *" for 3 AM daily

# Retention policy
retention_days = 30
max_backups = 10  # Keep at most 10, even if newer than retention

# Destination
destination = "~/.local/share/wa/backups/"

# Options
compress = true
metadata_only = false  # Set true for quick backups

# Notifications
notify_on_failure = true
notify_on_success = false
```

## Behavior
- Backup runs as part of `wa watch` (if enabled)
- Uses same export logic as `wa backup export`
- Rotation: delete oldest when max_backups exceeded
- Retry: attempt 3 times with exponential backoff on failure
- Status visible in `wa status`:
  ```
  Scheduled backup: enabled (daily)
    Last backup: 2026-01-18T03:00:15 (45 MB)
    Next backup: 2026-01-19T03:00:00
    Backups kept: 8/10
  ```

## Implementation Notes
- Use tokio cron scheduler or simple timer
- Lock coordination: brief pause of writes during export
- Fail gracefully: never crash watcher if backup fails

## Testing
- Unit tests: schedule parsing, rotation logic
- Integration: trigger scheduled backup, verify rotation
- E2E: run watcher with backup enabled, verify files created

## Acceptance Criteria
- Backup runs on configured schedule
- Rotation respects both retention_days and max_backups
- Status shows last/next backup times
- Failures logged and optionally notified

 {"id":"bd-a0c.1","title":"WeztermInterface trait: abstraction layer for real and mock implementations","description":"# WeztermInterface trait

## Purpose
Create an abstraction layer over WezTerm interactions that allows swapping between real CLI and mock implementations.

## Current State
wa-core directly calls WezTerm CLI commands. This needs to be abstracted.

## Interface Design
```rust
#[async_trait]
pub trait WeztermInterface: Send + Sync {
    /// List all panes across all windows
    async fn list_panes(&self) -> Result<Vec<PaneInfo>>;
    
    /// Get text from a pane's scrollback
    async fn get_text(&self, pane_id: u64, options: &GetTextOptions) -> Result<String>;
    
    /// Send text to a pane (keystrokes)
    async fn send_text(&self, pane_id: u64, text: &str) -> Result<()>;
    
    /// Spawn a new pane
    async fn spawn(&self, options: &SpawnOptions) -> Result<PaneInfo>;
    
    /// Split a pane
    async fn split(&self, pane_id: u64, direction: SplitDirection) -> Result<PaneInfo>;
    
    /// Activate (focus) a pane
    async fn activate(&self, pane_id: u64) -> Result<()>;
    
    /// Get pane info by ID
    async fn get_pane(&self, pane_id: u64) -> Result<Option<PaneInfo>>;
}

pub struct GetTextOptions {
    pub start_line: Option<i64>,
    pub end_line: Option<i64>,
    pub escapes: bool,
}

pub struct SpawnOptions {
    pub domain: Option<String>,
    pub cwd: Option<PathBuf>,
    pub command: Option<Vec<String>>,
}

pub enum SplitDirection {
    Horizontal,
    Vertical,
}
```

## Real Implementation
```rust
pub struct WeztermCli {
    socket_path: Option<PathBuf>,
    timeout: Duration,
}

#[async_trait]
impl WeztermInterface for WeztermCli {
    async fn list_panes(&self) -> Result<Vec<PaneInfo>> {
        let output = Command::new("wezterm")
            .args(["cli", "list", "--format", "json"])
            .output()
            .await?;
        // Parse JSON...
    }
    // ... other methods
}
```

## Factory Pattern
```rust
pub fn create_wezterm(config: &Config) -> Box<dyn WeztermInterface> {
    if config.simulate {
        Box::new(MockWezterm::new())
    } else {
        Box::new(WeztermCli::new(&config.wezterm))
    }
}
```

## Migration Path
1. Define trait
2. Implement for existing WeztermCli
3. Update callers to use trait
4. Implement MockWezterm (separate task)

## Testing
- Trait method contract tests
- Real implementation tests (with WezTerm)
- Factory tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] WeztermInterface trait covers all operations
- [ ] WeztermCli implements trait
- [ ] All existing code migrated to use trait
- [ ] Factory creates appropriate implementation
- [ ] Tests verify trait contract","notes":"Migrated remaining WeztermClient call sites in wa-core/wa to WeztermHandle (default_wezterm_handle) + WeztermHandleSource for waits. WorkflowRunner/WorkflowContext now use PolicyGatedInjector<WeztermHandle> alias; tests updated. WeztermInterface gains circuit_status(). cargo fmt/check/clippy/test all pass.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:52:49.54500413Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-07T02:22:29.525502911Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T02:22:29.525376256Z"}
 {"id":"bd-am5","title":"E2E script: dry-run mode (wa send/workflow --dry-run previews)","description":"# E2E script: dry-run mode (previews for wa send / wa workflow / robot)

## Goal
Validate end-to-end that `--dry-run` produces accurate previews **without mutating anything**:
- correct target resolution (pane/domain/cwd/agent)
- correct policy evaluation (allow/deny/require-approval)
- correct “what would happen” step plan for workflows
- deterministic, schema-valid JSON output for machines

This is the integration proof for the dry-run epic (`wa-1pe`) using the shared E2E harness.

## Why this matters
Dry-run is the primary trust-building UX feature:
- users can explore safely
- robots can plan before acting
- reduces mistakes and support burden

## Key constraints
- **Zero side effects**:
  - must not send input to panes
  - must not acquire workflow locks
  - must not mark events handled
  - must not create workflow executions
  - may optionally write an audit entry tagged as a *dry-run evaluation* (explicitly non-action)
- **Deterministic**: no fixed sleeps; use bounded waits/quiescence.
- **Machine-stable**: tests should prefer `--format json` outputs (avoid scraping rich/TTY output).
- Uses the standard harness contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).

## Test setup
- Start a WezTerm mux server and spawn dummy panes that:
  - can echo any received input to prove whether a send occurred
  - can deterministically emit patterns to create events for workflow previews
- Run `wa watch` in an isolated E2E workspace.

## Scenarios

### 1) `wa send --dry-run` preview quality
- Run: `wa send --pane 3 --dry-run "hello" --format json`
- Assert preview includes:
  - resolved pane info (pane_id, title, cwd, agent)
  - policy decision + reasons (capabilities, safety gate)
  - an explicit “would send” description (bytes/line endings/control codes)
  - an explicit “would wait-for” plan if applicable

### 2) `wa send --dry-run` on a denied state
- Create a deterministic unsafe state (fixture / dummy alt-screen).
- Run: `wa send --pane 3 --dry-run "hello" --format json`
- Assert:
  - decision is deny/require-approval as expected
  - the preview explains remediation (e.g., exit AltScreen)
  - **no text was sent** (dummy pane echo confirms)

### 3) Preview vs actual: stable-field match
Goal: the preview’s *decision* and *target* must match the actual path.
- Capture preview JSON: `wa send … --dry-run --format json > artifacts/send_preview.json`
- Execute actual send: `wa send … --format json > artifacts/send_actual.json`
- Compare only stable fields:
  - `target.pane_id`
  - `policy.decision` + `policy.rule_id` (if present)
  - `action_kind`
  - (ignore timestamps, durations, correlation ids)

### 4) `wa workflow run --dry-run` step plan preview
- Ensure a deterministic unhandled event exists (dummy pane emits marker).
- Run: `wa workflow run handle_compaction --pane 3 --dry-run --format json`
- Assert preview includes:
  - step list in order (with step ids/names)
  - per-step policy checks (or a summarized policy gate)
  - expected waits/verifications
  - explicit note that no locks will be acquired and no DB state will change

### 5) Robot dry-run output (schema + no mutation)
- Run: `wa robot send --pane 3 --dry-run "hello" --format json`
- Run: `wa robot workflow handle_compaction 3 --dry-run --format json` (or the canonical robot workflow invocation)
- Assert outputs validate against the dry-run JSON schemas and include:
  - `decision` / `would_execute=false`
  - `steps_preview` (for workflows)

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`) with prerequisites and default inclusion status.

## Artifacts
- `send_preview.json`
- `send_actual.json`
- `workflow_preview.json`
- `dry_run_vs_actual.json` (comparison result)
- `wa_watch.log`
- `events.jsonl`
- `audit_slice.jsonl` (to prove “no action” vs “action”)

## Logging contract
Log per scenario:
- command invoked (redacted)
- timeout settings
- comparison summary (stable fields)

Example:
```
[DRYRUN_E2E] scenario=send_preview pane=3
[DRYRUN_E2E] decision=allow would_execute=false
[DRYRUN_E2E] scenario=preview_vs_actual stable_match=true
```

## Testing
- Determinism:
  - no fixed sleeps
  - every wait has a timeout and emits actionable failure output
- No-mutation proofs (pick at least two independent signals):
  - dummy pane did not echo received input
  - no new `send_text` audit action exists (only optional `dry_run` audit)
  - no `workflow_executions` row was created on dry-run
- Schema:
  - JSON outputs validate against the documented dry-run schema(s)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dry-run output is informative (target + policy + plan).
- [ ] Dry-run never mutates panes/locks/handled state.
- [ ] Preview decisions match actual decisions on stable fields.
- [ ] Robot dry-run output is schema-valid and deterministic.
- [ ] Artifacts/logging make failures easy to debug.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:11:33.257051753Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-06T03:32:32.007499983Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T03:32:32.007423661Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-am5","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-am5","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
